description: A hot key expires and all concurrent requests recompute it at once, multiplying origin load until the backend degrades.
body: Failure mode: a hot key expires and every concurrent request recomputes it simultaneously, multiplying origin load until the backend slows or falls over. Detection signals: periodic origin load spikes aligned with TTL boundaries; latency cliffs on popular keys; database connection saturation at expiry. Do not let unprotected hot keys expire under load. Safer fallback: add a single-flight recompute lock with a timeout, serve stale-while-revalidate during refill, and stagger TTLs with jitter.
